description:
## Compromised workstation – malware activity

Investigation on a compromised workstation found at institution ABC.

tasks_template.description:
- Isolate the affected workstation and prevent further spread.
- Disconnect the host from the network (EDR network isolation, switch port shutdown, VPN disconnect).
- Instruct the user to stop using the device.
- Record who reported the incident and when.

subtasks.description: Isolate the workstation from the network.
Contact the user and instruct them to stop using the device.

tasks_template.title: Immediate containment

tasks_template.description:
Collect volatile and non-volatile evidence from the compromised workstation.
- Capture relevant logs (system, security, EDR, proxy if available).
- Preserve suspicious files and artefacts for analysis.
- Avoid unnecessary reboots until evidence collection is complete.

subtasks.description: Export EDR and system logs for the affected endpoint.
Collect suspicious binaries, scripts and documents.

tasks_template.title: Evidence collection

tasks_template.description:
Identify the malware family and capabilities.
- Perform static and dynamic analysis in a controlled environment.
- Extract indicators (hashes, domains, IPs, mutexes, registry keys).
- Document persistence mechanisms and command and control behaviour.

subtasks.description: Document indicators of compromise and behaviours.

tasks_template.title: Malware analysis

tasks_template.description:
Assess whether the compromise extends beyond the initial workstation.
- Review authentication logs, EDR alerts and network telemetry for lateral movement.
- Identify any additional systems or accounts that may be affected.
- Evaluate the likelihood of data exfiltration.

subtasks.description: Hunt for related activity on other endpoints and servers.
Check for suspicious use of privileged accounts.

tasks_template.title: Scope and impact assessment

tasks_template.description:
Remove the malware and restore the workstation safely.
- Remove malicious files and persistence artefacts or reimage the system according to policy.
- Apply missing security updates and hardening measures.
- Validate that the system is clean before returning it to production.

subtasks.description: Execute eradication procedure or perform full reimage.
Verify that malware activity no longer occurs.

tasks_template.title: Eradication and recovery

tasks_template.description:
Share indicators and context with MISP or an internal threat repository.
- Create or update a MISP event with indicators from the case.
- Apply appropriate TLP and distribution settings.
- Link the Flowintel case ID in the MISP event for traceability.

subtasks.description: Publish indicators of compromise to MISP.
Map indicators to relevant malware or intrusion-set galaxies.

tasks_template.title: Update MISP / threat repository

tasks_template.description:
Capture lessons learned and close the case.
- Summarise root cause, detection quality and response timeline.
- Propose improvements (rules, hardening, awareness, processes).
- Attach the final report and update case status.

subtasks.description: Draft and attach the final incident report.
Log agreed follow-up actions for tracking in separate tasks or cases.

tasks_template.title: Post-incident review

title: Compromised workstation
